title: Document doctor's restart-offer behavior in README and commit
action: In README.md, in the '### Check and configure with `doctor`' section (around line 56, right after the sentence ending '...no separate `config` command, no flags to remember.'), add one sentence: doctor now offers to restart the running background service right after it generates a fresh login token, so the new token takes effect immediately, and that the manual '### Restart' section further down still applies whenever a setting or secret is changed by hand or through the settings editor (which does not trigger this offer). Do not restructure or remove any existing content -- this is an additive one-sentence documentation note for behavior that already shipped (feature doctor-restart-offer, cell doctor-restart-offer-1, capped and merged into docs/specs/doctor.md). After editing, stage and commit README.md with a conventional commit message (docs: ...) that includes the cell id 'readme-doctor-restart-note-1', following this repo's commit convention (see recent git log for style) -- no AI references in the message.

truths: the added sentence accurately describes shipped behavior only (matches docs/specs/doctor.md's R8/behavior block: restart is offered only after a fresh token generation, confirm-gated, never automatic)
no existing README content is removed, reworded, or restructured beyond the one additive sentence
the manual '### Restart' section is left intact (it still documents the manual path for non-doctor-triggered restarts)
README.md is committed with a conventional commit message containing the cell id 'readme-doctor-restart-note-1', no AI references in the message
key_links: docs/specs/doctor.md R8 and 'Offer to restart the running service' behavior block
prohibitions: no change to any file other than README.md
no removal or rewording of the existing '### Restart' section
no AI/session references in the commit message

verify: grep -q 'offers to restart' README.md && git log -1 --format=%B | grep -q readme-doctor-restart-note-1

trace.outcome: Added documentation sentence about doctor's restart-offer behavior in README.md
trace.verify_output: grep -q 'offers to restart' README.md: found; git log shows cell id in commit message
trace.verify_command: grep -q 'offers to restart' README.md && git log -1 --format=%B | grep -q readme-doctor-restart-note-1
